summary: Follow-up improvements discovered while reconciling llm_wiki_vnext_detailed_review_report_ko.docx against the current repository state.

observations.problem: codex_exec role execution and wrapper mutation/check command execution still have no per-process timeout or timeout-specific result contract even though the auto-improve session has a max_minutes budget.
observations.why_it_matters: A hung role, mutation, or repo-health process can consume the session budget without producing clear timeout telemetry or a bounded failure mode for routing and promotion review.
observations.automation_candidate: Add executor and wrapper command timeout configuration, pass timeout to subprocess execution, classify TimeoutExpired as a blocking report/result, and record timeout_seconds plus termination_reason in schema-backed telemetry and timeout failure artifacts.
observations.suggested_followup: Keep timeout defaults aligned with session budget, and extend the same timeout result fields plus timeout-failure artifact writer if new executor adapters or command runners are added.

observations.surface: ops/scripts/behavior_delta_runtime.py, ops/scripts/mechanism_run_workspace_runtime.py, ops/scripts/promotion_gate_mechanism_runtime.py, ops/scripts/mechanism_run_validation_runtime.py, ops/scripts/planning_gate_validate_runtime.py, ops/scripts/observability_artifacts_runtime.py, ops/policies/wiki-maintainer-policy.yaml, ops/schemas/behavior-delta.schema.json, tests/test_behavior_delta_runtime.py, tests/test_promotion_gate_equal_score.py, tests/test_run_mechanism_experiment.py
observations.problem: Mechanism assessment and equal-score promotion used to lean on structural metrics, lint, test count, and aggregate eval score rather than a dedicated behavior-delta artifact.
observations.why_it_matters: A candidate can preserve structure and aggregate score while changing behavior in a way that is hard to diagnose after promotion.
observations.automation_candidate: Add a schema-backed behavior-delta artifact generated from changed-files manifest plus baseline/candidate workspace comparison, connect it to promotion inputs, planning validation, telemetry, and artifact fingerprinting.
observations.suggested_followup: Deterministic behavior-delta artifact generation, declared-scope intent/contract-touch annotations, policy flag defaults, WARN-on-manual-missing behavior, auto-improve required behavior, and same-eval promotion hard gate are automated. Future work can add explicit policy-backed regression classification once there is a reviewed regression ontology.

observations.problem: The original run_mechanism_experiment_runtime monolith has been decomposed, mechanism promotion report assembly now has state/rule/report helpers, auto-improve session rollup, proposal queue selection, execution outcome handling, and run_auto_improve_session phase boundaries are extracted, mechanism review history loading plus candidate emission/calibration are extracted, scaffold template payload generation is extracted, scaffold command/input resolution is extracted, registry lint support/alignment/review-candidate passes are extracted behind a thin wiki_lint_registry_runtime facade, and policy contract validation now separates fail-closed safety invariants from data-driven registry reference checks. Remaining decomposition risk is now concentrated in narrower orchestration surfaces such as scaffold starter/load orchestration.
observations.why_it_matters: Large orchestration modules increase review cost for self-modifying runtime changes and make it harder to isolate safety-critical regressions.
observations.automation_candidate: Continue extracting remaining orchestration surfaces into smaller typed services only where pass-level or failure-path contracts become clearer.
observations.suggested_followup: Promotion gate mechanism assembly is partially automated via state/rule/report helpers. Auto-improve session rollup now lives in auto_improve_session_runtime.py, proposal queue selection now lives in auto_improve_queue_runtime.py, dataclass-based execution outcome handling now lives in auto_improve_outcome_runtime.py, and run_auto_improve_session now delegates refresh/select, route/scaffold, execute/evaluate, and persist boundaries to phase result helpers. Mechanism review history loading now lives in mechanism_review_history_runtime.py, mechanism review candidate emission plus historical/session calibration now live in mechanism_review_candidate_runtime.py, scaffold template payloads now live in mechanism_run_scaffold_templates_runtime.py, command/input resolution now lives in mechanism_run_scaffold_resolution_runtime.py with focused failure-path tests, registry lint support/alignment/review-candidate passes now live in dedicated modules with direct contract tests, and policy contract validation now has explicit safety-invariant and registry-reference helpers. Next reassess scaffold starter/load orchestration and promotion decision registry downstream alignment. Auto-improve phase helpers can remain in-module until independent fault injection or external reuse justifies another file split.

observations.problem: The package and CI still require Python 3.14 even though the ops Python sources parse under Python 3.12 grammar in a static syntax check.
observations.why_it_matters: A 3.14-only public mirror raises contributor setup cost unless the project documents a concrete runtime dependency on 3.14 or tests compatibility across a lower supported floor.
observations.automation_candidate: Either document the 3.14-only rationale and bootstrap expectations, or run a 3.12/3.13 compatibility probe and lower requires-python if the full test suite supports it.
observations.suggested_followup: Python 3.12 compatibility was verified with compileall, focused tests, fast serial tests, public mirror checks, slow/integration tiers, and package install/build simulation before lowering requires-python to >=3.12. CI now covers both Python 3.12 floor and Python 3.14 current runtime.

observations.surface: ops/scripts/filesystem_runtime.py, ops/scripts/finalize_run_runtime.py, ops/scripts/set_mechanism_run_history.py, tests/test_filesystem_runtime.py, tests/test_finalize_run.py, tests/test_set_mechanism_run_history.py
observations.problem: atomic_multi_write accepted duplicate target paths inside a single transaction and silently let the last write win.
observations.why_it_matters: Batched write helpers should fail closed on ambiguous target sets so callers do not accidentally mask earlier content or rely on order-dependent overwrite behavior.
observations.automation_candidate: Canonicalize transaction targets before staging, reject duplicate destinations with a hard error, and pin the contract with direct regression coverage.
observations.suggested_followup: atomic_multi_write now rejects duplicate canonical targets before staging any files, using resolved-target identity with OS-native case normalization rather than lexical path spelling. finalize_run_runtime and set_mechanism_run_history now construct their transaction lists through small module-local batch helpers backed by build_atomic_text_updates from simple (path, text) specs instead of hand-rolled dictionaries with staging internals. Keep future batched-write callers under that same builder contract, and only add stronger path-policy checks in higher-level surfaces that actually own repo boundary/symlink rules.
